entities.name: V
entities.context: The person whose worldview belief system this document represents

entities.name: Disciplined restraint
entities.context: Core principle that restraint rather than maximal effort enables optimal long-term physiological and skill growth

entities.name: Plateau of over-exertion
entities.context: Phenomenon where pushing to absolute limits early triggers stagnation and prevents foundational conditioning

entities.name: Non-linear adaptation curve
entities.context: Model describing how growth follows recovery-driven structural change rather than linear effort-output

entities.name: Consistency over intensity
entities.context: Strategic principle that founders and athletes should optimize for steady effort over bursts

entities.name: Strategic under-performance
entities.context: Intentional short-term restraint as prerequisite for elite long-term performance

entities.name: Heart-rate variability
entities.context: Physiological marker used to measure recovery and cellular adaptation

entities.name: Mitochondrial density
entities.context: Biological marker of cellular adaptation affected by recovery cycles

relationships.context: V holds this as a core worldview principle for growth
V advocates this as optimization strategy for founders and athletes
V sees short-term restraint as prerequisite for elite performance
Disciplined restraint prevents hitting the plateau of over-exertion
Understanding non-linear adaptation justifies strategic under-performance
Both are complementary principles for long-term optimization
HRV is a measurable indicator of the adaptation process
